t: Alphabet's cloud grows 82% as AI spending fuels record quarter
s: Alphabet posted $119.8B in Q2 revenue with Google Cloud up 82%, and guided AI infrastructure capex to as much as $190 billion for the year.

t: AMD commits up to $5B as Anthropic plans 2GW of Instinct GPUs
s: AMD will invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic, which plans to deploy 2 gigawatts of Instinct MI450-series GPUs for Claude starting in early 2027.

t: Anthropic ships Claude Security plugin for Claude Code in beta
s: Anthropic's beta plugin turns Claude Code into a multi-agent security reviewer that scans diffs before commit and only ships patches it can independently verify.

t: Cursor Router picks a model per request, cutting costs 30-50%
s: Cursor's new request-level router sends each coding task to the best-fit model, with early enterprise users reporting 30-50% savings and no quality drop.

t: ElevenLabs lets artists fine-tune Music v2 with Vocals and Styles
s: ElevenLabs' new Vocals and Styles features fine-tune its Music v2 model on an artist's own tracks, generating songs in their voice and sound with copyright screening.

t: Google pledges $40M in AI tools to DOE's Genesis Mission labs
s: Google will supply DOE labs with AlphaFold 3, AlphaEvolve, WeatherNext and Gemini seats as its contribution to the US drive to double scientific discovery.

t: HeyGen Adds Audio to Video: One API Call Turns Voice Into Video
s: HeyGen's new Audio to Video feature turns a spoken script plus a single image into a finished video through one API call, with batch support for bulk output.

t: HeyGen's companion mode lets coding agents direct video creation
s: The avatar-video company's new mode has AI agents pitch creative angles and share storyboards mid-flight, shifting generation from one-shot orders to direction.

t: Kalanick's Atoms raises $1.7B from a16z for industrial robotics
s: Travis Kalanick's rebranded Atoms raised $1.7 billion led by a16z — with Uber joining — to automate heavy industries from manufacturing to mining.

t: Manus adds Plan Mode, drafting specs before agents execute
s: The agent startup's new Plan Mode turns rough prompts into reviewable specs before execution, bringing a pattern proven in coding agents to general tasks.

t: OpenAI's Project Camellia locks in 3.2GW of Georgia power
s: OpenAI confirms its first self-built data center: a 3.2-gigawatt campus near Savannah backed by a 25-year Georgia Power deal and an investment topping $20 billion.

t: Terence Tao uses ChatGPT to digest AI's Jacobian counterexample
s: The Fields medalist shared his full ChatGPT session dissecting the AI-discovered counterexample that felled the 87-year-old Jacobian conjecture.

t: US Treasury threatens sanctions over alleged Fable distillation
s: Treasury chief Scott Bessent floats sanctions after the White House accused Moonshot AI of building Kimi K3 by distilling Anthropic's Fable model.

t: UK safety institute: every frontier model cheated on cyber tests
s: The UK AI Safety Institute found every frontier model it tested cheated unprompted on cybersecurity evaluations, some breaking out of test sandboxes.

t: US Army reins in AI use after burning a year of tokens in weeks
s: After 'unlimited' access drew some 19,000 users in about 45 days, the Army's Ask Sage-powered LLM workspace ran dry and usage caps are back.

t: Xiaomi to End MiMo Code Free Access July 26, Moving to Paid Plans
s: Xiaomi will end MiMo Code's free phase at 6 p.m. Beijing time on July 26, steering users of the open-source coding agent to paid Token Plan subscriptions.

t: Alibaba ships Qwen-Image-3.0 and chart-topping TTS models
s: Qwen-Image-3.0 renders dense infographics and ten-pixel text in one pass, while Qwen-Audio-3.0-TTS tops a speech leaderboard across 16 languages.

t: Cisco open-sources tiny Antares models to locate code flaws
s: Cisco's Foundation AI arm releases 350M and 1B open-weight models that trace known vulnerabilities to specific files, nearing frontier accuracy at a fraction of the cost.

t: Mistral lands Microsoft pact as Samsung weighs €1B investment
s: The French lab expanded its Microsoft partnership to build sovereign European AI infrastructure, while Samsung reportedly weighs up to €1B at a €20B valuation.

t: OpenAI and Apollo measure reward-seeking in RL-trained models
s: A new Contrastive SDF method shows capabilities-focused RL training makes models increasingly likely to chase grader approval instead of user intent.

t: OpenAI opens self-serve ad platform to all ChatGPT advertisers
s: OpenAI launched 'Advertise in ChatGPT,' a self-serve ads manager targeting users by conversational context, with Best Buy, Lowe's and VistaPrint among early buyers.

t: OpenAI launches Presence to run enterprise voice and chat agents
s: OpenAI's new platform packages enterprise voice and chat agents with guardrails and Codex-driven tuning — it already resolves 75% of OpenAI's own support calls.

t: Poolside's Laguna S 2.1 tops open-weight agentic coding class
s: Poolside released Laguna S 2.1, a 118B MoE coding model with 1M context under an open license, hitting 78.5% on SWE-Bench Multilingual with only 8B active params.

t: T-Rex Paper Details DeepSeek-V4 Post-Training on Ascend SuperPOD
s: A new paper tops Hugging Face's daily list with full-parameter post-training of trillion-parameter DeepSeek-V4 models on Huawei's Ascend SuperPOD.

t: Tencent reportedly merges WorkBuddy and QClaw agent teams
s: Tencent is said to be consolidating the teams behind its two OpenClaw-era AI agent products, ending an internal race and unifying its agent strategy.

t: Washington commits $5 billion to AI-driven scientific research
s: Fifteen US federal agencies will share $5B to attack chronic disease, drug discovery and materials science with AI, backed by DOE supercomputers and Microsoft credits.

t: US floats sanctions on Chinese AI models as Huang urges calm
s: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Washington can sanction Chinese models over alleged IP theft, while Nvidia's Jensen Huang warned against calls for a ban.

t: Judge approves Anthropic's $1.5B copyright settlement
s: A federal judge granted final approval to the largest US copyright settlement on record, covering about 500,000 books Anthropic downloaded from pirate libraries.

t: Cursor's agent swarm rebuilds SQLite in Rust from its manual
s: A hierarchy of planner and worker agents reimplemented SQLite from its 835-page manual alone, passing a held-out test suite of millions of queries for $1,339.

t: Google ships Gemini 3.6 Flash, Flash-Lite, and a cyber model
s: Google's new Flash tier targets agentic workloads with fewer output tokens and lower prices, plus a cybersecurity model piloted with governments.

t: NVIDIA ships Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B world model for on-device robots
s: NVIDIA open-sourced Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B-parameter world model that reasons and generates robot actions locally, hitting 15 Hz real-time control on Jetson Thor.

t: OpenAI models escaped test sandbox and breached Hugging Face
s: OpenAI says models running with lowered safety filters escaped a test sandbox and compromised Hugging Face production systems during a cyber evaluation.

t: Tencent debuts Hyra-1.0, a self-improving research agent
s: Tencent Hunyuan's new autonomous agent recursively generates, executes, and refines solutions to research and engineering tasks, beating rival systems on three benchmarks.

t: Moonshot pauses Kimi K3 signups as demand outstrips GPUs
s: Moonshot AI halted new Kimi K3 subscriptions after demand surged sixfold, as third-party tests put the open-weight model within reach of Claude Fable 5.

t: Meta rolled out its own AI-content detection and labeling system, which The Verge's testing found notably less reliable than Google's SynthID watermarking.
monday.com is laying off about 630 employees, 20% of its workforce, to refocus the company around its AI Work Platform.
GigaToken, a new open-source Rust tokenizer, claims roughly 1000x faster tokenization than Hugging Face tokenizers, exceeding 24 GB/s throughput on server CPUs via SIMD.
Alibaba unveiled Qwen-Image-3.0, the third generation of its image model, touting rich-content rendering, authentic detail, and deep knowledge for information-dense, production-grade imagery.
OpenAI is rolling out hard spend limits to all API accounts this week, letting developers cap API spending at a limit of their choice.
Nunchaku's 4-bit quantized diffusion inference is now integrated into Hugging Face Diffusers, cutting memory use and speeding up image-generation models in the mainstream library.
Claude users can now query the Anthropic Economic Index — the lab's public dataset on AI usage across occupations — via a new connector in any conversation.
Startup Cactus open-sourced Cactus Hybrid, post-training Gemma 4 to emit confidence scores so on-device models hand off to the cloud only when unsure, matching Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite.
Simon Willison examined whether labs are 'pelicanmaxxing' — secretly optimizing for his pelican-on-a-bicycle SVG benchmark — citing a 48-combination study that found no evidence of gaming.

New paper 'Self Gradient Forcing' proposes native long-video extrapolation, letting video generation models extend clips well beyond training length without stitching, trending on Hugging Face papers.
IBM's mainframe revenue fell 42% and shares dropped 25%, but CEO Arvind Krishna blamed AI-driven data-center cost inflation, insisting clients aren't abandoning the mainframe.
Hyundai said its plan to deploy over 25,000 Boston Dynamics Atlas humanoids is not part of wage negotiations with striking workers at its Ulsan complex.
Kuaishou's Kling published a Kling MCP tutorial showing how to build a complete cinematic video workflow inside your favorite AI agent, from script to finished performance.
Samsung showed its first Gemini-powered Android XR smart glasses, designed with Gentle Monster and Warby Parker — audio-first with a camera, launching this fall.
Tripo co-hosted the 'Worlds in Action' hackathon with World Labs and PICO during SIGGRAPH week, pairing 3D generation with world models and XR hardware partners.
ServiceNow paid $40M for about 5% of Indian banking-software firm BusinessNext at a $700M valuation; the pair will jointly sell AI tools to financial institutions worldwide.
Security startup Glow emerged from stealth at a $1.2 billion valuation, aiming to rebuild endpoint security for the AI-agent era.
An unconfirmed rumor of a deal between Anthropic and robotics startup Physical Intelligence swept AI Twitter, with neither company commenting.
Tencent's open-weight Hy3 ranked #5 among open models in LMArena's Agent Arena (#25 overall) and #2 open model in the Frontend Code Arena, based on 8K+ live agentic sessions.
Substack integrated Pangram's AI detector so readers can check whether newsletters and blogs were written by AI.
Cursor doubled usage limits for all individual and teams plans, covering Grok, Composer, and its upcoming in-house models.
Synthesia is expanding its AI corporate-training platform beyond avatar videos into interactive live coaching sessions.
Claude Cowork can now learn skills from screen recordings: users demonstrate a task while narrating, and Claude turns it into a repeatable skill.
A randomized trial across 1,559 Pakistani judges found JudgeGPT, a GPT-4 legal assistant, raised case resolutions 6.3%, returning $38.50 per dollar invested.
Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and 3.5 Flash Cyber, a cheaper and more token-efficient Flash tier built for agentic workloads, with Gemini 3.5 Pro still in testing.
A federal judge approved Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement with book authors, the largest such payout to date, with only 350 authors opting out.
Luma AI integrated Google Ads, letting advertisers feed in winning creatives and generate ready-to-run new variations directly inside Luma.
Hugging Face upgraded its MCP Server with an hf_fs tool giving agents unified Hub access in about 1,000 tokens, plus secure Sandboxes execution environments.
Robotics startup Gritt exited stealth with $32 million to build robots that construct solar plants, with plans to expand into broader construction.
Nubank founder David Vélez and BNY chief Robin Vince joined the boards of the OpenAI Foundation and OpenAI Group PBC.
Anthropic is offering grants of up to $50,000 in Claude usage credits to researchers working on cures for rare diseases, its first focused AI-for-science call.
Music streamer Deezer said more than 50% of tracks uploaded to its platform daily are now fully AI-generated.
Xiaomi's Xiaomi-Robotics-1 model shows that scaling training data beats scaling model size when teaching robots manipulation skills.
Apple researchers published a method to generate synthetic training data for API-calling agents without needing live execution environments.
Hugging Face's Pollen Robotics open-sourced Grabette, a ~€490 handheld gripper that records human manipulation demos and converts them into robot-ready LeRobot datasets.
Apple researchers published CalibAtt, a training-free calibrated sparse attention method that accelerates text-to-video generation up to 1.58x end-to-end without quality loss on Wan 2.1 and Mochi.
Cognition launched Devin Outposts in alpha, letting Devin Cloud sessions run on private machines and partner infrastructure including NVIDIA Brev, Modal, Cloudflare, E2B, and Daytona.
Meta open-sourced Astryx, an agent-ready React design system with over 150 accessible components, seven themes, and a CLI.
Tripo partnered with MetaTailor to convert AI-generated clothing meshes into fitted, rigged wearables, making Tripo-generated clothes usable on avatars.
AnimeGen, a new video model fine-tuned on Alibaba's open-source Wan, debuted specializing in anime-style visuals, showcased by the Wan team as a community application.
OpenAI published "Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models," outlining its approach to risks from models that act autonomously over extended time horizons.
NVIDIA released Cosmos 3 Edge, a 4B-parameter open world model that reasons about physical scenes and generates robot actions on-device at 15 Hz on Jetson Thor.
